The text may be for a completely new article (see [[Help:New article]]), or to expand an existing English Wikipedia article. If text is copied over from another Wiki then appropriate attribution must be placed in the edit history of the article (see the guide line [[Wikipedia:Copying within Wikipedia#Translating from other language Wikimedia projects|Copying within Wikipedia §Translating from other language Wikimedia projects]]) This help page does not currently cover how to translate from English Wikipedia to a foreign language. With the exception of quotations, use normal [[Wikipedia:Manual of Style#Clarity|English encyclopedic style]], as appropriate for the topic. ===Section headings=== {{Further|Wikipedia:Manual of Style#Section headings}} The acceptable style for section headings may vary between articles in different languages, as may the layout of the appendix sections. With the exception of quotations, use normal [[Wikipedia:Manual of Style#Clarity|English encyclopedic style]], as appropriate for the topic. ===Section headings=== {{Further|Wikipedia:Manual of Style#Section headings}} The acceptable style for section headings may vary between articles in different languages, as may the layout of the appendix sections. On English Wikipedia the style of section headings is described in the [[Wikipedia:Manual of Style|Manual of Style]] (MOS) guideline. For example section headers should normally not start with "A", "An", or "The", and the heading ought to be presented in [[sentence case]] ({{xt|Funding of UNESCO projects in developing countries}}), not title case ({{!xt|Funding of UNESCO Projects in Developing Countries}})—see the MOS sections: [[MOS:HEADINGS|Section headings]] and indirectly [[MOS:AT|Article titles]] for guidance. ===Layout and ordering=== {{Further|Wikipedia:Manual of Style#Section organization|Wikipedia:Manual of Style/Layout}} Although the ordering of information in the main body of an article is not described in detail in the MOS—for example whether the family of a subject of a biography is mentioned in passing in the chronological order of the subject's life, or is placed in a separate section, is left to the discretion of the editors. However there is guidance in the MOS on the appendices of an article. These include section header names, the ordering of the sections and content (see the MOS guideline [[MOS:APPENDIX|Layout § Standard appendices and footers]]). For example the section "See also", if included, ought to contain a bulleted list of [[Wikipedia:Manual of Style/Linking|internal links]] to related Wikipedia articles and be placed before the notes and references sections. ==Citation templates== As you translate an article, you may encounter citation templates in the original such as {{((}}[[:fr:Template:lien web|''lien web'']]{{))}} in a French article, or {{((}}[[:es:Template:cita libro|''cita libro'']]{{))}} in Spanish which have citation parameters in the source language, for example: * {{lien web |auteur= |titre= |éditeur= |pages= |url= |...}} – [[:fr:Template:lien web|French cite web]] template * {{cita libro |autor= |título= |editorial= |páginas= |url= |...}} – [[:es:Template:cita libro|Spanish cite book]] template For [[List of Wikipedias#Wikipedia editions|most languages]], you will have to manually transate the foreign parameter names into the correct parameter name used by the English citation template. However, a shortcut is available in some cases, where you can just paste the foreign citation template as is into the English article, and it will render correctly. This is handled by the [[Module:CS1 translator|CS1 translator]] module, which can translate four of the most popular templates (cite book, cite web, cite journal, cite news) into English for [[Module:CS1 translator#Supported templates|fourteen languages]]: * Arabic, Catalan, Danish, Dutch, Finnish, French, German, Italian, Norwegian, Polish, Portuguese, Spanish, Swedish, Turkish. If the non-English citation template is supported by a translator (see [[Module:CS1 translator#Supported templates|list]]), all you need to do is copy the citation from the source and paste it into the en-wiki article, preview, fix any errors, and publish. In general, the auto-translator will produce a correctly formatted CS1|2 template from the source that will get substituted by AnomieBot. The translations are not perfect so please make sure that the rendered result is correct. In addition, please add the {{para|language}} parameter to the citation template using the [[WP:LANGCODE|Wikipedia language code]]; e.g. add {{para|language|fr}} for a French source, {{para|language|de}} for a German source, and so on. ==Tools== ===Content Translation=== [[File:Content Translation Screencast (English).webm|thumb|500px|Screencast showing how to use [[Special:ContentTranslation|Content Translation]]|right]] The [[Special:ContentTranslation|Content Translation tool]] may be used to facilitate the translation process. This tool is currently limited to editors with [[Wikipedia:Extended confirmed editors|extended confirmed]] rights (automatically granted to accounts with 30 days tenure and at least 500 edits). Please see more information at [[Wikipedia:Content translation tool]]. {{clr}} ===Automatic translation=== {{anchor|Machine translation}}{{shortcut|WP:MACHINE|WP:MACHINETRANSLATION}} Translation takes work. [[Machine translation]] can vary considerably in quality of translation from language to language, and should be used with caution. Machine translation can have difficulty with individual words or expressions for many reasons, including [[false friends]], [[false cognates]], [[literal translation]], [[neologisms]], [[slang]], [[idiomatic expression]]s and other unrecognized compounds, [[Polysemy|words with multiple meanings]], words with [[Jargon|specialized meanings in certain knowledge domains]] different from a common meaning of a word in a more general context, unwarranted translation of proper names, and other reasons. Wikipedia consensus is that '''an unedited machine translation''', left as a Wikipedia article, is worse than nothing. (This is partly because translation templates automatically carry links to machine translations, so readers can easily access machine translations anyway.) [[Category:refreshing s 1-26]][[en:Translation]]‎]] [[Category:refreshing s 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing s 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing s 1-26]][[Category:refreshing s 1-26]][[Category:refreshing s 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing s 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing s 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing s 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing s 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing s 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing s 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing s 1-26]][[en:Translation]][[Category:refreshing s 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing s 1-26]][[Category:refreshing s 1-26]][[Category:refreshing s 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing s 1-26]][[Category:refreshing s 1-26]][[Category:refreshing s 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing s 1-26]][[Category:refreshing s 1-26]][[Category:refreshing s 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing s 1-26]][[en:Translation]][[Category:refreshing s 1-26]] [[Category:Pages tagged as templates in userspace Help]]

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text: {{Short description|Wikimedia Project Page}} {{See also|Wikipedia:Translation}} {{Wikipedia how to|WP:HOWTRANS|HELP:HOWTRANS}} {{Merge to|Wikipedia:Translation|discuss=Wikipedia talk:Translation#Merge proposal}} This is a help page that describes how to copy and translate text from a [[Wikipedia:About#Other languages|Wikipedia foreign language]] article into English. The text may be for a completely new article (see [[Help:New article]]), or to expand an existing English Wikipedia article. If text is copied over from another Wiki then appropriate attribution must be placed in the edit history of the article (see the guide line [[Wikipedia:Copying within Wikipedia#Translating from other language Wikimedia projects|Copying within Wikipedia §Translating from other language Wikimedia projects]]) This help page does not currently cover how to translate from English Wikipedia to a foreign language. For help with that see [[Wikipedia:Translate us]]. ==Policy requirements== ===Content=== {{Main|Wikipedia:Core content policies}} {{Further|WP:Neutral point of view|WP:No original research}} {{See also|Wikipedia:List of content guidelines}} All English Wikipedia articles must meet the requirements of the three core content policies [[Wikipedia:No original research|no original research]], [[Wikipedia:Neutral point of view|neutral point of view]] and [[Wikipedia:Verifiability|verifiability]]. This is particularly true for [[Wikipedia:Biographies of living persons|biographies of living people]]. In addition articles must also comply with the [[Wikipedia:Copyrights|copyright policy]]. Articles translated from other languages are expected to meet English Wikipedia's [[WP:N|notability guidelines]]; simply having an article on another Wikipedia project does not establish notability by itself. If portions of an article appear to be low-quality or unverifiable, use your judgment and do not translate those portions. Once you have finished translating, you may ask a [[:Category:kk]][[Category:refreshing s 1-26]] to check the translation. ====Verifiability: Include citations==== {{Main|WP:Verifiability}} Include the same sources that the original article used, if they are appropriate. For example, if you're not translating some text in the original article because you think it's irrelevant, then omit the related citations, or find other text in the article that they support. (Sources are valuable.) Do check to see if an English-language citation is available that is the equivalent of the foreign-language citation (for example an authorized translation), since that's easier for readers to verify. If you do find one, you don't need to include the foreign-language citation. If you use a translated edition of a book, make sure to adjust the page numbers to those in the translation. If you translate just the beginning of an article, and the foreign language lead is unsourced because the references supporting it are in the body, you ought to import those body references, inserting them into the translated lead at the appropriate locations to meet [[WP:Verifiability|verifiability]] requirements. Many foreign language articles do not meet the levels of [[WP:Verifiability|verifiability]] that is expected in English Wikipedia articles. If an article has sections with no citations then follow the guidance in [[Wikipedia:UNSOURCED|UNSOURCED]] and either provide additional inline sources, or add appropriate citation needed templates as described in [[Wikipedia:UNSOURCED|UNSOURCED]]. === Licensing === {{Main|Wikipedia:Copyrights}} ==== Attribution ==== {{Main|Wikipedia:Copying within Wikipedia}} A translation is a derivative work. Wikipedia articles are under a [[copyleft]] license that requires attribution to the original source and authors in all derivative works. How to do this is described on [[Wikipedia:Copying within Wikipedia#Translating from other language Wikimedia projects|Translating from other language Wikimedia projects]]. '''Required''' – the new, translated article must credit the source article: :*(a) Add a statement to the [[Help:Edit summary|edit summary]] of the target article of your translation providing [[WP:TFOLWP|translation attribution]] to the authors of the source article, including an [[Help:Interlanguage links|interlanguage link]] to the source (translated-from) article. Example: :** Content in this edit is translated from the existing French Wikipedia article at [[:fr:''Exact name of French article'']]; see its history for attribution. :: (See also [[Wikipedia:Copying within Wikipedia]], and its section at [[WP:RIA]] if you find a translation that failed to provide mandatory copyright attribution.) '''Optional''' – you may also: :*(b) Place the {{tl|Translated page}} template on the target article's talk page, for example: :** {{Translated page|fr|''Exact name of the French article''}} – the template also takes some additional optional parameters). :*(c) Import the revision history of the source into the translated article. If you wish to do so, ask at [[Wikipedia:Requests for page importation]] for the page to either be imported into your userspace or over your translated version of the article. ==Style== {{Further|Wikipedia:Manual of style}} Articles on a given subject in different languages are typically edited independently, and need not correspond closely in form, style or content. ===Jargon=== {{Main|Wikipedia:Manual of Style#Technical language}} The English text should be [[Wikipedia:Make technical articles understandable|understandable to a wide audience]], so – other things being equal – use everyday English expressions rather than [[jargon]] or foreign expressions. It may be necessary to add material explaining terms or cultural concepts unfamiliar to English-speaking readers. ===Quotations=== {{Further|Wikipedia:Manual of Style#Quotations}} Avoid being overly influenced by the style of the original. With the exception of quotations, use normal [[Wikipedia:Manual of Style#Clarity|English encyclopedic style]], as appropriate for the topic. ===Section headings=== {{Further|Wikipedia:Manual of Style#Section headings}} The acceptable style for section headings may vary between articles in different languages, as may the layout of the appendix sections. On English Wikipedia the style of section headings is described in the [[Wikipedia:Manual of Style|Manual of Style]] (MOS) guideline. For example section headers should normally not start with "A", "An", or "The", and the heading ought to be presented in [[sentence case]] ({{xt|Funding of UNESCO projects in developing countries}}), not title case ({{!xt|Funding of UNESCO Projects in Developing Countries}})—see the MOS sections: [[MOS:HEADINGS|Section headings]] and indirectly [[MOS:AT|Article titles]] for guidance. ===Layout and ordering=== {{Further|Wikipedia:Manual of Style#Section organization|Wikipedia:Manual of Style/Layout}} Although the ordering of information in the main body of an article is not described in detail in the MOS—for example whether the family of a subject of a biography is mentioned in passing in the chronological order of the subject's life, or is placed in a separate section, is left to the discretion of the editors. However there is guidance in the MOS on the appendices of an article. These include section header names, the ordering of the sections and content (see the MOS guideline [[MOS:APPENDIX|Layout § Standard appendices and footers]]). For example the section "See also", if included, ought to contain a bulleted list of [[Wikipedia:Manual of Style/Linking|internal links]] to related Wikipedia articles and be placed before the notes and references sections. ==Citation templates== As you translate an article, you may encounter citation templates in the original such as {{((}}[[:fr:Template:lien web|''lien web'']]{{))}} in a French article, or {{((}}[[:es:Template:cita libro|''cita libro'']]{{))}} in Spanish which have citation parameters in the source language, for example: * {{lien web |auteur= |titre= |éditeur= |pages= |url= |...}} – [[:fr:Template:lien web|French cite web]] template * {{cita libro |autor= |título= |editorial= |páginas= |url= |...}} – [[:es:Template:cita libro|Spanish cite book]] template For [[List of Wikipedias#Wikipedia editions|most languages]], you will have to manually transate the foreign parameter names into the correct parameter name used by the English citation template. However, a shortcut is available in some cases, where you can just paste the foreign citation template as is into the English article, and it will render correctly. This is handled by the [[Module:CS1 translator|CS1 translator]] module, which can translate four of the most popular templates (cite book, cite web, cite journal, cite news) into English for [[Module:CS1 translator#Supported templates|fourteen languages]]: * Arabic, Catalan, Danish, Dutch, Finnish, French, German, Italian, Norwegian, Polish, Portuguese, Spanish, Swedish, Turkish. If the non-English citation template is supported by a translator (see [[Module:CS1 translator#Supported templates|list]]), all you need to do is copy the citation from the source and paste it into the en-wiki article, preview, fix any errors, and publish. In general, the auto-translator will produce a correctly formatted CS1|2 template from the source that will get substituted by AnomieBot. The translations are not perfect so please make sure that the rendered result is correct. In addition, please add the {{para|language}} parameter to the citation template using the [[WP:LANGCODE|Wikipedia language code]]; e.g. add {{para|language|fr}} for a French source, {{para|language|de}} for a German source, and so on. ==Tools== ===Content Translation=== [[File:Content Translation Screencast (English).webm|thumb|500px|Screencast showing how to use [[Special:ContentTranslation|Content Translation]]|right]] The [[Special:ContentTranslation|Content Translation tool]] may be used to facilitate the translation process. This tool is currently limited to editors with [[Wikipedia:Extended confirmed editors|extended confirmed]] rights (automatically granted to accounts with 30 days tenure and at least 500 edits). Please see more information at [[Wikipedia:Content translation tool]]. {{clr}} ===Automatic translation=== {{anchor|Machine translation}}{{shortcut|WP:MACHINE|WP:MACHINETRANSLATION}} Translation takes work. [[Machine translation]] can vary considerably in quality of translation from language to language, and should be used with caution. Machine translation can have difficulty with individual words or expressions for many reasons, including [[false friends]], [[:en:False cognates|False cognates]], [[literal translation]], [[neologisms]], [[slang]], [[idiomatic expression]]s and other unrecognized compounds, [[Polysemy|words with multiple meanings]], words with [[Jargon|specialized meanings in certain knowledge domains]] different from a common meaning of a word in a more general context, unwarranted translation of proper names, and other reasons. Wikipedia consensus is that '''an unedited machine translation''', left as a Wikipedia article, is worse than nothing. (This is partly because translation templates automatically carry links to machine translations, so readers can easily access machine translations anyway.) [[Category:refreshing s 1-26]][[en:Translation]]‎]] [[Category:refreshing s 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing s 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing s 1-26]][[Category:refreshing s 1-26]][[Category:refreshing s 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing s 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing s 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing s 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing s 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing s 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing s 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing s 1-26]][[en:Translation]][[Category:refreshing s 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing s 1-26]][[Category:refreshing s 1-26]][[Category:refreshing s 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing s 1-26]][[Category:refreshing s 1-26]][[Category:refreshing s 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing s 1-26]][[Category:refreshing s 1-26]][[Category:refreshing s 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing s 1-26]][[en:Translation]][[Category:refreshing s 1-26]] [[Category:Pages tagged as templates in userspace Help]]

Do check to see if an English-language citation is available that is the equivalent of the foreign-language citation (for example an authorized translation), since that's easier for readers to verify. If you do find one, you don't need to include the foreign-language citation. If you use a translated edition of a book, make sure to adjust the page numbers to those in the translation. If you translate just the beginning of an article, and the foreign language lead is unsourced because the references supporting it are in the body, you ought to import those body references, inserting them into the translated lead at the appropriate locations to meet [[WP:Verifiability|verifiability]] requirements. Many foreign language articles do not meet the levels of [[WP:Verifiability|verifiability]] that is expected in English Wikipedia articles. If an article has sections with no citations then follow the guidance in [[Wikipedia:UNSOURCED|UNSOURCED]] and either provide additional inline sources, or add appropriate citation needed templates as described in [[Wikipedia:UNSOURCED|UNSOURCED]]. === Licensing === {{Main|Wikipedia:Copyrights}} ==== Attribution ==== {{Main|Wikipedia:Copying within Wikipedia}} A translation is a derivative work. Wikipedia articles are under a [[copyleft]] license that requires attribution to the original source and authors in all derivative works. How to do this is described on [[Wikipedia:Copying within Wikipedia#Translating from other language Wikimedia projects|Translating from other language Wikimedia projects]]. '''Required''' – the new, translated article must credit the source article: :*(a) Add a statement to the [[Help:Edit summary|edit summary]] of the target article of your translation providing [[WP:TFOLWP|translation attribution]] to the authors of the source article, including an [[Help:Interlanguage links|interlanguage link]] to the source (translated-from) article. Example: :** Content in this edit is translated from the existing French Wikipedia article at [[:fr:''Exact name of French article'']]; see its history for attribution. :: (See also [[Wikipedia:Copying within Wikipedia]], and its section at [[WP:RIA]] if you find a translation that failed to provide mandatory copyright attribution.) '''Optional''' – you may also: :*(b) Place the {{tl|Translated page}} template on the target article's talk page, for example: :** {{Translated page|fr|''Exact name of the French article''}} – the template also takes some additional optional parameters). :*(c) Import the revision history of the source into the translated article. If you wish to do so, ask at [[Wikipedia:Requests for page importation]] for the page to either be imported into your userspace or over your translated version of the article. ==Style== {{Further|Wikipedia:Manual of style}} Articles on a given subject in different languages are typically edited independently, and need not correspond closely in form, style or content. ===Jargon=== {{Main|Wikipedia:Manual of Style#Technical language}} The English text should be [[Wikipedia:Make technical articles understandable|understandable to a wide audience]], so – other things being equal – use everyday English expressions rather than [[jargon]] or foreign expressions. It may be necessary to add material explaining terms or cultural concepts unfamiliar to English-speaking readers. ===Quotations=== {{Further|Wikipedia:Manual of Style#Quotations}} Avoid being overly influenced by the style of the original. With the exception of quotations, use normal [[Wikipedia:Manual of Style#Clarity|English encyclopedic style]], as appropriate for the topic. ===Section headings=== {{Further|Wikipedia:Manual of Style#Section headings}} The acceptable style for section headings may vary between articles in different languages, as may the layout of the appendix sections. On English Wikipedia the style of section headings is described in the [[Wikipedia:Manual of Style|Manual of Style]] (MOS) guideline. For example section headers should normally not start with "A", "An", or "The", and the heading ought to be presented in [[sentence case]] ({{xt|Funding of UNESCO projects in developing countries}}), not title case ({{!xt|Funding of UNESCO Projects in Developing Countries}})—see the MOS sections: [[MOS:HEADINGS|Section headings]] and indirectly [[MOS:AT|Article titles]] for guidance. ===Layout and ordering=== {{Further|Wikipedia:Manual of Style#Section organization|Wikipedia:Manual of Style/Layout}} Although the ordering of information in the main body of an article is not described in detail in the MOS—for example whether the family of a subject of a biography is mentioned in passing in the chronological order of the subject's life, or is placed in a separate section, is left to the discretion of the editors. However there is guidance in the MOS on the appendices of an article. These include section header names, the ordering of the sections and content (see the MOS guideline [[MOS:APPENDIX|Layout § Standard appendices and footers]]). For example the section "See also", if included, ought to contain a bulleted list of [[Wikipedia:Manual of Style/Linking|internal links]] to related Wikipedia articles and be placed before the notes and references sections. ==Citation templates== As you translate an article, you may encounter citation templates in the original such as {{((}}[[:fr:Template:lien web|''lien web'']]{{))}} in a French article, or {{((}}[[:es:Template:cita libro|''cita libro'']]{{))}} in Spanish which have citation parameters in the source language, for example: * {{lien web |auteur= |titre= |éditeur= |pages= |url= |...}} – [[:fr:Template:lien web|French cite web]] template * {{cita libro |autor= |título= |editorial= |páginas= |url= |...}} – [[:es:Template:cita libro|Spanish cite book]] template For [[List of Wikipedias#Wikipedia editions|most languages]], you will have to manually transate the foreign parameter names into the correct parameter name used by the English citation template. However, a shortcut is available in some cases, where you can just paste the foreign citation template as is into the English article, and it will render correctly. This is handled by the [[Module:CS1 translator|CS1 translator]] module, which can translate four of the most popular templates (cite book, cite web, cite journal, cite news) into English for [[Module:CS1 translator#Supported templates|fourteen languages]]: * Arabic, Catalan, Danish, Dutch, Finnish, French, German, Italian, Norwegian, Polish, Portuguese, Spanish, Swedish, Turkish. If the non-English citation template is supported by a translator (see [[Module:CS1 translator#Supported templates|list]]), all you need to do is copy the citation from the source and paste it into the en-wiki article, preview, fix any errors, and publish. In general, the auto-translator will produce a correctly formatted CS1|2 template from the source that will get substituted by AnomieBot. The translations are not perfect so please make sure that the rendered result is correct. In addition, please add the {{para|language}} parameter to the citation template using the [[WP:LANGCODE|Wikipedia language code]]; e.g. add {{para|language|fr}} for a French source, {{para|language|de}} for a German source, and so on. ==Tools== ===Content Translation=== [[File:Content Translation Screencast (English).webm|thumb|500px|Screencast showing how to use [[Special:ContentTranslation|Content Translation]]|right]] The [[Special:ContentTranslation|Content Translation tool]] may be used to facilitate the translation process. This tool is currently limited to editors with [[Wikipedia:Extended confirmed editors|extended confirmed]] rights (automatically granted to accounts with 30 days tenure and at least 500 edits). Please see more information at [[Wikipedia:Content translation tool]]. {{clr}} ===Automatic translation=== {{anchor|Machine translation}}{{shortcut|WP:MACHINE|WP:MACHINETRANSLATION}} Translation takes work. [[Machine translation]] can vary considerably in quality of translation from language to language, and should be used with caution. Machine translation can have difficulty with individual words or expressions for many reasons, including [[:en:False friends|False friends]], [[:en:False cognates|False cognates]], [[literal translation]], [[neologisms]], [[slang]], [[idiomatic expression]]s and other unrecognized compounds, [[Polysemy|words with multiple meanings]], words with [[Jargon|specialized meanings in certain knowledge domains]] different from a common meaning of a word in a more general context, unwarranted translation of proper names, and other reasons. Wikipedia consensus is that '''an unedited machine translation''', left as a Wikipedia article, is worse than nothing. (This is partly because translation templates automatically carry links to machine translations, so readers can easily access machine translations anyway.) [[Category:refreshing s 1-26]][[en:Translation]]‎]] [[Category:refreshing s 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing s 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing s 1-26]][[Category:refreshing s 1-26]][[Category:refreshing s 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing s 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing s 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing s 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing s 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing s 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing s 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing s 1-26]][[en:Translation]][[Category:refreshing s 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing s 1-26]][[Category:refreshing s 1-26]][[Category:refreshing s 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing s 1-26]][[Category:refreshing s 1-26]][[Category:refreshing s 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing s 1-26]][[Category:refreshing s 1-26]][[Category:refreshing s 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing s 1-26]][[en:Translation]][[Category:refreshing s 1-26]]

revision.text: {{Short description|Wikimedia Project Page}} {{See also|Wikipedia:Translation}} {{Wikipedia how to|WP:HOWTRANS|HELP:HOWTRANS}} {{Merge to|Wikipedia:Translation|discuss=Wikipedia talk:Translation#Merge proposal}} This is a help page that describes how to copy and translate text from a [[Wikipedia:About#Other languages|Wikipedia foreign language]] article into English. The text may be for a completely new article (see [[Help:New article]]), or to expand an existing English Wikipedia article. If text is copied over from another Wiki then appropriate attribution must be placed in the edit history of the article (see the guide line [[Wikipedia:Copying within Wikipedia#Translating from other language Wikimedia projects|Copying within Wikipedia §Translating from other language Wikimedia projects]]) This help page does not currently cover how to translate from English Wikipedia to a foreign language. For help with that see [[Wikipedia:Translate us]]. ==Policy requirements== ===Content=== {{Main|Wikipedia:Core content policies}} {{Further|WP:Neutral point of view|WP:No original research}} {{See also|Wikipedia:List of content guidelines}} All English Wikipedia articles must meet the requirements of the three core content policies [[Wikipedia:No original research|no original research]], [[Wikipedia:Neutral point of view|neutral point of view]] and [[Wikipedia:Verifiability|verifiability]]. This is particularly true for [[Wikipedia:Biographies of living persons|biographies of living people]]. In addition articles must also comply with the [[Wikipedia:Copyrights|copyright policy]]. Articles translated from other languages are expected to meet English Wikipedia's [[WP:N|notability guidelines]]; simply having an article on another Wikipedia project does not establish notability by itself. If portions of an article appear to be low-quality or unverifiable, use your judgment and do not translate those portions. Once you have finished translating, you may ask a [[:Category:kk]][[Category:refreshing s 1-26]] to check the translation. ====Verifiability: Include citations==== {{Main|WP:Verifiability}} Include the same sources that the original article used, if they are appropriate. For example, if you're not translating some text in the original article because you think it's irrelevant, then omit the related citations, or find other text in the article that they support. (Sources are valuable.) Do check to see if an English-language citation is available that is the equivalent of the foreign-language citation (for example an authorized translation), since that's easier for readers to verify. If you do find one, you don't need to include the foreign-language citation. If you use a translated edition of a book, make sure to adjust the page numbers to those in the translation. If you translate just the beginning of an article, and the foreign language lead is unsourced because the references supporting it are in the body, you ought to import those body references, inserting them into the translated lead at the appropriate locations to meet [[WP:Verifiability|verifiability]] requirements. Many foreign language articles do not meet the levels of [[WP:Verifiability|verifiability]] that is expected in English Wikipedia articles. If an article has sections with no citations then follow the guidance in [[Wikipedia:UNSOURCED|UNSOURCED]] and either provide additional inline sources, or add appropriate citation needed templates as described in [[Wikipedia:UNSOURCED|UNSOURCED]]. === Licensing === {{Main|Wikipedia:Copyrights}} ==== Attribution ==== {{Main|Wikipedia:Copying within Wikipedia}} A translation is a derivative work. Wikipedia articles are under a [[:en:Copyleft|Copyleft]] license that requires attribution to the original source and authors in all derivative works. '''Required''' – the new, translated article must credit the source article: :*(a) Add a statement to the [[Help:Edit summary|edit summary]] of the target article of your translation providing [[WP:TFOLWP|translation attribution]] to the authors of the source article, including an [[Help:Interlanguage links|interlanguage link]] to the source (translated-from) article. Example: :** Content in this edit is translated from the existing French Wikipedia article at [[:fr:''Exact name of French article'']]; see its history for attribution. :: (See also [[Wikipedia:Copying within Wikipedia]], and its section at [[WP:RIA]] if you find a translation that failed to provide mandatory copyright attribution.) '''Optional''' – you may also: :*(b) Place the {{tl|Translated page}} template on the target article's talk page, for example: :** {{Translated page|fr|''Exact name of the French article''}} – the template also takes some additional optional parameters). :*(c) Import the revision history of the source into the translated article. If you wish to do so, ask at [[Wikipedia:Requests for page importation]] for the page to either be imported into your userspace or over your translated version of the article. ==Style== {{Further|Wikipedia:Manual of style}} Articles on a given subject in different languages are typically edited independently, and need not correspond closely in form, style or content. ===Jargon=== {{Main|Wikipedia:Manual of Style#Technical language}} The English text should be [[Wikipedia:Make technical articles understandable|understandable to a wide audience]], so – other things being equal – use everyday English expressions rather than [[jargon]] or foreign expressions. It may be necessary to add material explaining terms or cultural concepts unfamiliar to English-speaking readers. ===Quotations=== {{Further|Wikipedia:Manual of Style#Quotations}} Avoid being overly influenced by the style of the original. With the exception of quotations, use normal [[Wikipedia:Manual of Style#Clarity|English encyclopedic style]], as appropriate for the topic. ===Section headings=== {{Further|Wikipedia:Manual of Style#Section headings}} The acceptable style for section headings may vary between articles in different languages, as may the layout of the appendix sections. On English Wikipedia the style of section headings is described in the [[Wikipedia:Manual of Style|Manual of Style]] (MOS) guideline. For example section headers should normally not start with "A", "An", or "The", and the heading ought to be presented in [[sentence case]] ({{xt|Funding of UNESCO projects in developing countries}}), not title case ({{!xt|Funding of UNESCO Projects in Developing Countries}})—see the MOS sections: [[MOS:HEADINGS|Section headings]] and indirectly [[MOS:AT|Article titles]] for guidance. ===Layout and ordering=== {{Further|Wikipedia:Manual of Style#Section organization|Wikipedia:Manual of Style/Layout}} Although the ordering of information in the main body of an article is not described in detail in the MOS—for example whether the family of a subject of a biography is mentioned in passing in the chronological order of the subject's life, or is placed in a separate section, is left to the discretion of the editors. (This is partly because translation templates automatically carry links to machine translations, so readers can easily access machine translations anyway.) The text may be for a completely new article (see [[Help:New article]]), or to expand an existing English Wikipedia article. If text is copied over from another Wiki then appropriate attribution must be placed in the edit history of the article (see the guide line [[Wikipedia:Copying within Wikipedia#Translating from other language Wikimedia projects|Copying within Wikipedia §Translating from other language Wikimedia projects]]) This help page does not currently cover how to translate from English Wikipedia to a foreign language. For help with that see [[Wikipedia:Translate us]]. ==Policy requirements== ===Content=== {{Main|Wikipedia:Core content policies}} {{Further|WP:Neutral point of view|WP:No original research}} {{See also|Wikipedia:List of content guidelines}} All English Wikipedia articles must meet the requirements of the three core content policies [[Wikipedia:No original research|no original research]], [[Wikipedia:Neutral point of view|neutral point of view]] and [[Wikipedia:Verifiability|verifiability]]. This is particularly true for [[Wikipedia:Biographies of living persons|biographies of living people]]. In addition articles must also comply with the [[Wikipedia:Copyrights|copyright policy]]. Articles translated from other languages are expected to meet English Wikipedia's [[WP:N|notability guidelines]]; simply having an article on another Wikipedia project does not establish notability by itself. If portions of an article appear to be low-quality or unverifiable, use your judgment and do not translate those portions. Once you have finished translating, you may ask a [[:Category:kk]] to check the translation. ====Verifiability: Include citations==== {{Main|WP:Verifiability}} Include the same sources that the original article used, if they are appropriate. For example, if you're not translating some text in the original article because you think it's irrelevant, then omit the related citations, or find other text in the article that they support. (Sources are valuable.) Do check to see if an English-language citation is available that is the equivalent of the foreign-language citation (for example an authorized translation), since that's easier for readers to verify. If you do find one, you don't need to include the foreign-language citation. If you use a translated edition of a book, make sure to adjust the page numbers to those in the translation. If you translate just the beginning of an article, and the foreign language lead is unsourced because the references supporting it are in the body, you ought to import those body references, inserting them into the translated lead at the appropriate locations to meet [[WP:Verifiability|verifiability]] requirements. Many foreign language articles do not meet the levels of [[WP:Verifiability|verifiability]] that is expected in English Wikipedia articles. If an article has sections with no citations then follow the guidance in [[Wikipedia:UNSOURCED|UNSOURCED]] and either provide additional inline sources, or add appropriate citation needed templates as described in [[Wikipedia:UNSOURCED|UNSOURCED]]. === Licensing === {{Main|Wikipedia:Copyrights}} ==== Attribution ==== {{Main|Wikipedia:Copying within Wikipedia}} A translation is a derivative work. Wikipedia articles are under a [[:en:Copyleft|Copyleft]] license that requires attribution to the original source and authors in all derivative works. How to do this is described on [[Wikipedia:Copying within Wikipedia#Translating from other language Wikimedia projects|Translating from other language Wikimedia projects]]. '''Required''' – the new, translated article must credit the source article: :*(a) Add a statement to the [[Help:Edit summary|edit summary]] of the target article of your translation providing [[WP:TFOLWP|translation attribution]] to the authors of the source article, including an [[Help:Interlanguage links|interlanguage link]] to the source (translated-from) article. Example: :** Content in this edit is translated from the existing French Wikipedia article at [[:fr:''Exact name of French article'']]; see its history for attribution. :: (See also [[Wikipedia:Copying within Wikipedia]], and its section at [[WP:RIA]] if you find a translation that failed to provide mandatory copyright attribution.) '''Optional''' – you may also: :*(b) Place the {{tl|Translated page}} template on the target article's talk page, for example: :** {{Translated page|fr|''Exact name of the French article''}} – the template also takes some additional optional parameters). :*(c) Import the revision history of the source into the translated article. If you wish to do so, ask at [[Wikipedia:Requests for page importation]] for the page to either be imported into your userspace or over your translated version of the article. ==Style== {{Further|Wikipedia:Manual of style}} Articles on a given subject in different languages are typically edited independently, and need not correspond closely in form, style or content. ===Jargon=== {{Main|Wikipedia:Manual of Style#Technical language}} The English text should be [[Wikipedia:Make technical articles understandable|understandable to a wide audience]], so – other things being equal – use everyday English expressions rather than [[jargon]] or foreign expressions. It may be necessary to add material explaining terms or cultural concepts unfamiliar to English-speaking readers. ===Quotations=== {{Further|Wikipedia:Manual of Style#Quotations}} Avoid being overly influenced by the style of the original. With the exception of quotations, use normal [[Wikipedia:Manual of Style#Clarity|English encyclopedic style]], as appropriate for the topic. ===Section headings=== {{Further|Wikipedia:Manual of Style#Section headings}} The acceptable style for section headings may vary between articles in different languages, as may the layout of the appendix sections. On English Wikipedia the style of section headings is described in the [[Wikipedia:Manual of Style|Manual of Style]] (MOS) guideline. For example section headers should normally not start with "A", "An", or "The", and the heading ought to be presented in [[sentence case]] ({{xt|Funding of UNESCO projects in developing countries}}), not title case ({{!xt|Funding of UNESCO Projects in Developing Countries}})—see the MOS sections: [[MOS:HEADINGS|Section headings]] and indirectly [[MOS:AT|Article titles]] for guidance. ===Layout and ordering=== {{Further|Wikipedia:Manual of Style#Section organization|Wikipedia:Manual of Style/Layout}} Although the ordering of information in the main body of an article is not described in detail in the MOS—for example whether the family of a subject of a biography is mentioned in passing in the chronological order of the subject's life, or is placed in a separate section, is left to the discretion of the editors. However there is guidance in the MOS on the appendices of an article. These include section header names, the ordering of the sections and content (see the MOS guideline [[MOS:APPENDIX|Layout § Standard appendices and footers]]). For example the section "See also", if included, ought to contain a bulleted list of [[Wikipedia:Manual of Style/Linking|internal links]] to related Wikipedia articles and be placed before the notes and references sections. ==Citation templates== As you translate an article, you may encounter citation templates in the original such as {{((}}[[:fr:Template:lien web|''lien web'']]{{))}} in a French article, or {{((}}[[:es:Template:cita libro|''cita libro'']]{{))}} in Spanish which have citation parameters in the source language, for example: * {{lien web |auteur= |titre= |éditeur= |pages= |url= |...}} – [[:fr:Template:lien web|French cite web]] template * {{cita libro |autor= |título= |editorial= |páginas= |url= |...}} – [[:es:Template:cita libro|Spanish cite book]] template For [[List of Wikipedias#Wikipedia editions|most languages]], you will have to manually transate the foreign parameter names into the correct parameter name used by the English citation template. However, a shortcut is available in some cases, where you can just paste the foreign citation template as is into the English article, and it will render correctly. This is handled by the [[Module:CS1 translator|CS1 translator]] module, which can translate four of the most popular templates (cite book, cite web, cite journal, cite news) into English for [[Module:CS1 translator#Supported templates|fourteen languages]]: * Arabic, Catalan, Danish, Dutch, Finnish, French, German, Italian, Norwegian, Polish, Portuguese, Spanish, Swedish, Turkish. If the non-English citation template is supported by a translator (see [[Module:CS1 translator#Supported templates|list]]), all you need to do is copy the citation from the source and paste it into the en-wiki article, preview, fix any errors, and publish. In general, the auto-translator will produce a correctly formatted CS1|2 template from the source that will get substituted by AnomieBot. The translations are not perfect so please make sure that the rendered result is correct. In addition, please add the {{para|language}} parameter to the citation template using the [[WP:LANGCODE|Wikipedia language code]]; e.g. add {{para|language|fr}} for a French source, {{para|language|de}} for a German source, and so on. ==Tools== ===Content Translation=== [[File:Content Translation Screencast (English).webm|thumb|500px|Screencast showing how to use [[Special:ContentTranslation|Content Translation]]|right]] The [[Special:ContentTranslation|Content Translation tool]] may be used to facilitate the translation process. This tool is currently limited to editors with [[Wikipedia:Extended confirmed editors|extended confirmed]] rights (automatically granted to accounts with 30 days tenure and at least 500 edits). Please see more information at [[Wikipedia:Content translation tool]]. {{clr}} ===Automatic translation=== {{anchor|Machine translation}}{{shortcut|WP:MACHINE|WP:MACHINETRANSLATION}} Translation takes work. [[Machine translation]] can vary considerably in quality of translation from language to language, and should be used with caution. Machine translation can have difficulty with individual words or expressions for many reasons, including [[:en:False friends|False friends]], [[:en:False cognates|False cognates]], [[literal translation]], [[neologisms]], [[slang]], [[:en:Idiomatic expression|Idiomatic expression]]s and other unrecognized compounds, [[Polysemy|words with multiple meanings]], words with [[Jargon|specialized meanings in certain knowledge domains]] different from a common meaning of a word in a more general context, unwarranted translation of proper names, and other reasons. Wikipedia consensus is that '''an unedited machine translation''', left as a Wikipedia article, is worse than nothing. (This is partly because translation templates automatically carry links to machine translations, so readers can easily access machine translations anyway.) [[en:Translation]]‎]] [[en:Translation]] [[en:Translation]]

The text may be for a completely new article (see [[Help:New article]]), or to expand an existing English Wikipedia article. If text is copied over from another Wiki then appropriate attribution must be placed in the edit history of the article (see the guide line [[Wikipedia:Copying within Wikipedia#Translating from other language Wikimedia projects|Copying within Wikipedia §Translating from other language Wikimedia projects]]) This help page does not currently cover how to translate from English Wikipedia to a foreign language. For help with that see [[Wikipedia:Translate us]]. ==Policy requirements== ===Content=== {{Main|Wikipedia:Core content policies}} {{Further|WP:Neutral point of view|WP:No original research}} {{See also|Wikipedia:List of content guidelines}} All English Wikipedia articles must meet the requirements of the three core content policies [[Wikipedia:No original research|no original research]], [[Wikipedia:Neutral point of view|neutral point of view]] and [[Wikipedia:Verifiability|verifiability]]. This is particularly true for [[Wikipedia:Biographies of living persons|biographies of living people]]. In addition articles must also comply with the [[Wikipedia:Copyrights|copyright policy]]. Articles translated from other languages are expected to meet English Wikipedia's [[WP:N|notability guidelines]]; simply having an article on another Wikipedia project does not establish notability by itself. If portions of an article appear to be low-quality or unverifiable, use your judgment and do not translate those portions. Once you have finished translating, you may ask a [[:Category:kk]] to check the translation. ====Verifiability: Include citations==== {{Main|WP:Verifiability}} Include the same sources that the original article used, if they are appropriate. For example, if you're not translating some text in the original article because you think it's irrelevant, then omit the related citations, or find other text in the article that they support. (Sources are valuable.) Do check to see if an English-language citation is available that is the equivalent of the foreign-language citation (for example an authorized translation), since that's easier for readers to verify. If you do find one, you don't need to include the foreign-language citation. If you use a translated edition of a book, make sure to adjust the page numbers to those in the translation. If you translate just the beginning of an article, and the foreign language lead is unsourced because the references supporting it are in the body, you ought to import those body references, inserting them into the translated lead at the appropriate locations to meet [[WP:Verifiability|verifiability]] requirements. Many foreign language articles do not meet the levels of [[WP:Verifiability|verifiability]] that is expected in English Wikipedia articles. If an article has sections with no citations then follow the guidance in [[Wikipedia:UNSOURCED|UNSOURCED]] and either provide additional inline sources, or add appropriate citation needed templates as described in [[Wikipedia:UNSOURCED|UNSOURCED]]. === Licensing === {{Main|Wikipedia:Copyrights}} ==== Attribution ==== {{Main|Wikipedia:Copying within Wikipedia}} A translation is a derivative work. Wikipedia articles are under a [[:en:Copyleft|Copyleft]] license that requires attribution to the original source and authors in all derivative works. How to do this is described on [[Wikipedia:Copying within Wikipedia#Translating from other language Wikimedia projects|Translating from other language Wikimedia projects]]. '''Required''' – the new, translated article must credit the source article: :*(a) Add a statement to the [[Help:Edit summary|edit summary]] of the target article of your translation providing [[WP:TFOLWP|translation attribution]] to the authors of the source article, including an [[Help:Interlanguage links|interlanguage link]] to the source (translated-from) article. Example: :** Content in this edit is translated from the existing French Wikipedia article at [[:fr:''Exact name of French article'']]; see its history for attribution. :: (See also [[Wikipedia:Copying within Wikipedia]], and its section at [[WP:RIA]] if you find a translation that failed to provide mandatory copyright attribution.) '''Optional''' – you may also: :*(b) Place the {{tl|Translated page}} template on the target article's talk page, for example: :** {{Translated page|fr|''Exact name of the French article''}} – the template also takes some additional optional parameters). :*(c) Import the revision history of the source into the translated article. If you wish to do so, ask at [[Wikipedia:Requests for page importation]] for the page to either be imported into your userspace or over your translated version of the article. ==Style== {{Further|Wikipedia:Manual of style}} Articles on a given subject in different languages are typically edited independently, and need not correspond closely in form, style or content. ===Jargon=== {{Main|Wikipedia:Manual of Style#Technical language}} The English text should be [[Wikipedia:Make technical articles understandable|understandable to a wide audience]], so – other things being equal – use everyday English expressions rather than [[:en:Jargon|Jargon]] or foreign expressions. It may be necessary to add material explaining terms or cultural concepts unfamiliar to English-speaking readers. ===Quotations=== {{Further|Wikipedia:Manual of Style#Quotations}} Avoid being overly influenced by the style of the original. With the exception of quotations, use normal [[Wikipedia:Manual of Style#Clarity|English encyclopedic style]], as appropriate for the topic. ===Section headings=== {{Further|Wikipedia:Manual of Style#Section headings}} The acceptable style for section headings may vary between articles in different languages, as may the layout of the appendix sections. On English Wikipedia the style of section headings is described in the [[Wikipedia:Manual of Style|Manual of Style]] (MOS) guideline. For example section headers should normally not start with "A", "An", or "The", and the heading ought to be presented in [[sentence case]] ({{xt|Funding of UNESCO projects in developing countries}}), not title case ({{!xt|Funding of UNESCO Projects in Developing Countries}})—see the MOS sections: [[MOS:HEADINGS|Section headings]] and indirectly [[MOS:AT|Article titles]] for guidance. ===Layout and ordering=== {{Further|Wikipedia:Manual of Style#Section organization|Wikipedia:Manual of Style/Layout}} Although the ordering of information in the main body of an article is not described in detail in the MOS—for example whether the family of a subject of a biography is mentioned in passing in the chronological order of the subject's life, or is placed in a separate section, is left to the discretion of the editors. However there is guidance in the MOS on the appendices of an article. These include section header names, the ordering of the sections and content (see the MOS guideline [[:en:MOS:APPENDIX|Layout § Standard appendices and footers]]). For example the section "See also", if included, ought to contain a bulleted list of [[Wikipedia:Manual of Style/Linking|internal links]] to related Wikipedia articles and be placed before the notes and references sections. ==Citation templates== As you translate an article, you may encounter citation templates in the original such as {{((}}[[:fr:Template:lien web|''lien web'']]{{))}} in a French article, or {{((}}[[:es:Template:cita libro|''cita libro'']]{{))}} in Spanish which have citation parameters in the source language, for example: * {{lien web |auteur= |titre= |éditeur= |pages= |url= |...}} – [[:fr:Template:lien web|French cite web]] template * {{cita libro |autor= |título= |editorial= |páginas= |url= |...}} – [[:es:Template:cita libro|Spanish cite book]] template For [[List of Wikipedias#Wikipedia editions|most languages]], you will have to manually transate the foreign parameter names into the correct parameter name used by the English citation template. However, a shortcut is available in some cases, where you can just paste the foreign citation template as is into the English article, and it will render correctly. This is handled by the [[Module:CS1 translator|CS1 translator]] module, which can translate four of the most popular templates (cite book, cite web, cite journal, cite news) into English for [[Module:CS1 translator#Supported templates|fourteen languages]]: * Arabic, Catalan, Danish, Dutch, Finnish, French, German, Italian, Norwegian, Polish, Portuguese, Spanish, Swedish, Turkish. If the non-English citation template is supported by a translator (see [[Module:CS1 translator#Supported templates|list]]), all you need to do is copy the citation from the source and paste it into the en-wiki article, preview, fix any errors, and publish. In general, the auto-translator will produce a correctly formatted CS1|2 template from the source that will get substituted by AnomieBot. The translations are not perfect so please make sure that the rendered result is correct. In addition, please add the {{para|language}} parameter to the citation template using the [[WP:LANGCODE|Wikipedia language code]]; e.g. add {{para|language|fr}} for a French source, {{para|language|de}} for a German source, and so on. ==Tools== ===Content Translation=== [[File:Content Translation Screencast (English).webm|thumb|500px|Screencast showing how to use [[Special:ContentTranslation|Content Translation]]|right]] The [[Special:ContentTranslation|Content Translation tool]] may be used to facilitate the translation process. This tool is currently limited to editors with [[Wikipedia:Extended confirmed editors|extended confirmed]] rights (automatically granted to accounts with 30 days tenure and at least 500 edits). Please see more information at [[Wikipedia:Content translation tool]]. {{clr}} ===Automatic translation=== {{anchor|Machine translation}}{{shortcut|WP:MACHINE|WP:MACHINETRANSLATION}} Translation takes work. [[Machine translation]] can vary considerably in quality of translation from language to language, and should be used with caution. Machine translation can have difficulty with individual words or expressions for many reasons, including [[:en:False friends|False friends]], [[:en:False cognates|False cognates]], [[:en:en:Literal translation|en:Literal translation]], [[neologisms]], [[slang]], [[:en:Idiomatic expression|Idiomatic expression]]s and other unrecognized compounds, [[Polysemy|words with multiple meanings]], words with [[:en:Jargon|specialized meanings in certain knowledge domains]] different from a common meaning of a word in a more general context, unwarranted translation of proper names, and other reasons. Wikipedia consensus is that '''an unedited machine translation''', left as a Wikipedia article, is worse than nothing. (This is partly because translation templates automatically carry links to machine translations, so readers can easily access machine translations anyway.) [[en:Translation]]‎]] [[en:Translation]] [[en:Translation]]

The text may be for a completely new article (see [[Help:New article]]), or to expand an existing English Wikipedia article. If text is copied over from another Wiki then appropriate attribution must be placed in the edit history of the article (see the guide line [[Wikipedia:Copying within Wikipedia#Translating from other language Wikimedia projects|Copying within Wikipedia §Translating from other language Wikimedia projects]]) This help page does not currently cover how to translate from English Wikipedia to a foreign language. For help with that see [[Wikipedia:Translate us]]. ==Policy requirements== ===Content=== {{Main|Wikipedia:Core content policies}} {{Further|WP:Neutral point of view|WP:No original research}} {{See also|Wikipedia:List of content guidelines}} All English Wikipedia articles must meet the requirements of the three core content policies [[Wikipedia:No original research|no original research]], [[Wikipedia:Neutral point of view|neutral point of view]] and [[Wikipedia:Verifiability|verifiability]]. This is particularly true for [[Wikipedia:Biographies of living persons|biographies of living people]]. In addition articles must also comply with the [[Wikipedia:Copyrights|copyright policy]]. Articles translated from other languages are expected to meet English Wikipedia's [[WP:N|notability guidelines]]; simply having an article on another Wikipedia project does not establish notability by itself. If portions of an article appear to be low-quality or unverifiable, use your judgment and do not translate those portions. Once you have finished translating, you may ask a [[:Category:kk]] to check the translation. ====Verifiability: Include citations==== {{Main|WP:Verifiability}} Include the same sources that the original article used, if they are appropriate. For example, if you're not translating some text in the original article because you think it's irrelevant, then omit the related citations, or find other text in the article that they support. (Sources are valuable.) Do check to see if an English-language citation is available that is the equivalent of the foreign-language citation (for example an authorized translation), since that's easier for readers to verify. If you do find one, you don't need to include the foreign-language citation. If you use a translated edition of a book, make sure to adjust the page numbers to those in the translation. If you translate just the beginning of an article, and the foreign language lead is unsourced because the references supporting it are in the body, you ought to import those body references, inserting them into the translated lead at the appropriate locations to meet [[WP:Verifiability|verifiability]] requirements. Many foreign language articles do not meet the levels of [[WP:Verifiability|verifiability]] that is expected in English Wikipedia articles. If an article has sections with no citations then follow the guidance in [[Wikipedia:UNSOURCED|UNSOURCED]] and either provide additional inline sources, or add appropriate citation needed templates as described in [[Wikipedia:UNSOURCED|UNSOURCED]]. === Licensing === {{Main|Wikipedia:Copyrights}} ==== Attribution ==== {{Main|Wikipedia:Copying within Wikipedia}} A translation is a derivative work. Wikipedia articles are under a [[:en:Copyleft|Copyleft]] license that requires attribution to the original source and authors in all derivative works. How to do this is described on [[Wikipedia:Copying within Wikipedia#Translating from other language Wikimedia projects|Translating from other language Wikimedia projects]]. '''Required''' – the new, translated article must credit the source article: :*(a) Add a statement to the [[Help:Edit summary|edit summary]] of the target article of your translation providing [[WP:TFOLWP|translation attribution]] to the authors of the source article, including an [[Help:Interlanguage links|interlanguage link]] to the source (translated-from) article. Example: :** Content in this edit is translated from the existing French Wikipedia article at [[:fr:''Exact name of French article'']]; see its history for attribution. :: (See also [[Wikipedia:Copying within Wikipedia]], and its section at [[WP:RIA]] if you find a translation that failed to provide mandatory copyright attribution.) '''Optional''' – you may also: :*(b) Place the {{tl|Translated page}} template on the target article's talk page, for example: :** {{Translated page|fr|''Exact name of the French article''}} – the template also takes some additional optional parameters). :*(c) Import the revision history of the source into the translated article. If you wish to do so, ask at [[Wikipedia:Requests for page importation]] for the page to either be imported into your userspace or over your translated version of the article. ==Style== {{Further|Wikipedia:Manual of style}} Articles on a given subject in different languages are typically edited independently, and need not correspond closely in form, style or content. ===Jargon=== {{Main|Wikipedia:Manual of Style#Technical language}} The English text should be [[Wikipedia:Make technical articles understandable|understandable to a wide audience]], so – other things being equal – use everyday English expressions rather than [[:en:Jargon|Jargon]] or foreign expressions. It may be necessary to add material explaining terms or cultural concepts unfamiliar to English-speaking readers. ===Quotations=== {{Further|Wikipedia:Manual of Style#Quotations}} Avoid being overly influenced by the style of the original. With the exception of quotations, use normal [[Wikipedia:Manual of Style#Clarity|English encyclopedic style]], as appropriate for the topic. ===Section headings=== {{Further|Wikipedia:Manual of Style#Section headings}} The acceptable style for section headings may vary between articles in different languages, as may the layout of the appendix sections. On English Wikipedia the style of section headings is described in the [[Wikipedia:Manual of Style|Manual of Style]] (MOS) guideline. For example section headers should normally not start with "A", "An", or "The", and the heading ought to be presented in [[sentence case]] ({{xt|Funding of UNESCO projects in developing countries}}), not title case ({{!xt|Funding of UNESCO Projects in Developing Countries}})—see the MOS sections: [[:en:MOS:HEADINGS|Section headings]] and indirectly [[:en:MOS:AT|Article titles]] for guidance. ===Layout and ordering=== {{Further|Wikipedia:Manual of Style#Section organization|Wikipedia:Manual of Style/Layout}} Although the ordering of information in the main body of an article is not described in detail in the MOS—for example whether the family of a subject of a biography is mentioned in passing in the chronological order of the subject's life, or is placed in a separate section, is left to the discretion of the editors. However there is guidance in the MOS on the appendices of an article. These include section header names, the ordering of the sections and content (see the MOS guideline [[:en:MOS:APPENDIX|Layout § Standard appendices and footers]]). For example the section "See also", if included, ought to contain a bulleted list of [[Wikipedia:Manual of Style/Linking|internal links]] to related Wikipedia articles and be placed before the notes and references sections. ==Citation templates== As you translate an article, you may encounter citation templates in the original such as {{((}}[[:fr:Template:lien web|''lien web'']]{{))}} in a French article, or {{((}}[[:es:Template:cita libro|''cita libro'']]{{))}} in Spanish which have citation parameters in the source language, for example: * {{lien web |auteur= |titre= |éditeur= |pages= |url= |...}} – [[:fr:Template:lien web|French cite web]] template * {{cita libro |autor= |título= |editorial= |páginas= |url= |...}} – [[:es:Template:cita libro|Spanish cite book]] template For [[List of Wikipedias#Wikipedia editions|most languages]], you will have to manually transate the foreign parameter names into the correct parameter name used by the English citation template. However, a shortcut is available in some cases, where you can just paste the foreign citation template as is into the English article, and it will render correctly. This is handled by the [[Module:CS1 translator|CS1 translator]] module, which can translate four of the most popular templates (cite book, cite web, cite journal, cite news) into English for [[Module:CS1 translator#Supported templates|fourteen languages]]: * Arabic, Catalan, Danish, Dutch, Finnish, French, German, Italian, Norwegian, Polish, Portuguese, Spanish, Swedish, Turkish. If the non-English citation template is supported by a translator (see [[Module:CS1 translator#Supported templates|list]]), all you need to do is copy the citation from the source and paste it into the en-wiki article, preview, fix any errors, and publish. In general, the auto-translator will produce a correctly formatted CS1|2 template from the source that will get substituted by AnomieBot. The translations are not perfect so please make sure that the rendered result is correct. In addition, please add the {{para|language}} parameter to the citation template using the [[WP:LANGCODE|Wikipedia language code]]; e.g. add {{para|language|fr}} for a French source, {{para|language|de}} for a German source, and so on. ==Tools== ===Content Translation=== [[File:Content Translation Screencast (English).webm|thumb|500px|Screencast showing how to use [[Special:ContentTranslation|Content Translation]]|right]] The [[Special:ContentTranslation|Content Translation tool]] may be used to facilitate the translation process. This tool is currently limited to editors with [[Wikipedia:Extended confirmed editors|extended confirmed]] rights (automatically granted to accounts with 30 days tenure and at least 500 edits). Please see more information at [[Wikipedia:Content translation tool]]. {{clr}} ===Automatic translation=== {{anchor|Machine translation}}{{shortcut|WP:MACHINE|WP:MACHINETRANSLATION}} Translation takes work. [[:en:Machine translation|Machine translation]] can vary considerably in quality of translation from language to language, and should be used with caution. Machine translation can have difficulty with individual words or expressions for many reasons, including [[:en:False friends|False friends]], [[:en:False cognates|False cognates]], [[:en:en:Literal translation|en:Literal translation]], [[neologisms]], [[slang]], [[:en:Idiomatic expression|Idiomatic expression]]s and other unrecognized compounds, [[Polysemy|words with multiple meanings]], words with [[:en:Jargon|specialized meanings in certain knowledge domains]] different from a common meaning of a word in a more general context, unwarranted translation of proper names, and other reasons. Wikipedia consensus is that '''an unedited machine translation''', left as a Wikipedia article, is worse than nothing. (This is partly because translation templates automatically carry links to machine translations, so readers can easily access machine translations anyway.) [[en:Translation]]‎]] [[en:Translation]] [[en:Translation]]
